title: Beef Stir-Fry with Honey Pepper Sauce

overview.description: Tender strips of beef stir-fried with vegetables in a glossy honey, soy and black pepper sauce.

ingredients: 500g beef steak, thinly sliced against the grain
1 tsp fresh ginger, finely chopped or grated
method: Slice the beef thinly against the grain.
Place the beef in a bowl and mix with 1 tbsp light soy sauce and 1 tsp cornflour.
Whisk together all the sauce ingredients.
Heat a large wok or frying pan over high heat.
Stir-fry the beef in batches until browned but not completely cooked through.
Remove the beef from the wok.
Stir-fry the onion, peppers and broccoli until beginning to soften while retaining some bite.
Stir-fry for approximately 30 seconds until fragrant.
Return the beef to the wok.
Stir-fry until the sauce comes to a simmer and thickens to a glossy consistency.
Cook until the beef is completely cooked through.

learning.did-you-know: Thinly slicing beef against the grain shortens the muscle fibres, making the meat much more tender when quickly stir-fried.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to prepare beef for quick high-heat cooking and how to build a glossy stir-fry sauce with a balance of sweet, salty, acidic and peppery flavours.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A quick, familiar and family-friendly stir-fry that makes good use of the sauces already in the pantry and provides a different style of beef meal from the longer-cooked dishes in the collection.
chapman-tips: Use a tender cut of beef and slice it very thinly.
Slice against the grain for the most tender result.
Cook the beef in batches so the wok stays hot.
Don't overcrowd the wok or the beef will steam instead of sear.
The black pepper should be noticeable but not necessarily hot.
Add extra pepper at the table for adults if desired.
Have every ingredient prepared before starting to cook.
The sauce thickens quickly, so add it only once the other ingredients are nearly ready.
notes.batch-cooking: Better fresh. The beef and vegetables are best immediately after stir-frying.
notes.leftovers: Good reheated gently, although the beef may become slightly firmer. Leftovers work well with noodles or rice.
